dataset: OpenClinica 3.13 Community Edition with sample_crf.xls (Vital Signs CRF template)

pi_items.source: Verified via setup_task.sh cleanup logic (DELETE FROM study WHERE unique_identifier='ONC-301') and export_result.sh query (SELECT ... FROM study WHERE unique_identifier='ONC-301'). The task description instructs agent to create study with this ID. Live test confirmed 0 studies with this ID exist after setup (check06_onc301.txt).
Stated in task description as 'Brief Title'. The export_result.sh checks study name via SQL query. This is a synthetic study name created for this task.
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh captures principal_investigator field from the study table. This is a synthetic PI name.
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh captures sponsor field. Synthetic company name.

Stated in task description as 'Protocol Type Interventional'. OpenClinica stores this in the protocol_type column of the study table. The export_result.sh captures it. OpenClinica 3.13 accepts 'interventional' and 'observational' as valid values.
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh queries expected_total_enrollment from the study table. This is a user-specified value, no external verification needed.
Stated in task description. OpenClinica stores facility info in study-level metadata. Note: this is also the site name (site_name metadata key), which is consistent.
Stated in task description ('in Boston, Massachusetts, United States'). Facility location stored in study table fields.
Stated in task description. Consistent with facility_city=Boston.
Stated in task description. Consistent with Boston, Massachusetts.
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh queries study_event_definition WHERE LOWER(name) LIKE '%screening%'. The setup_task.sh cleans up any pre-existing ONC-301 event definitions.
Stated in task description as 'Scheduled'. OpenClinica stores event type as 'Scheduled', 'Unscheduled', or 'Common'. The export_result.sh captures the type field.
Stated in task description as 'non-repeating'. OpenClinica stores this as a boolean in study_event_definition.repeating.
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh queries WHERE LOWER(name) LIKE '%baseline%'.
Stated in task description as 'Scheduled'.
Stated in task description as 'non-repeating'.
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh queries WHERE LOWER(name) LIKE '%cycle%1%day%1%'.
Stated in task description as 'Scheduled'.
Stated in task description as 'non-repeating'.

Verified via live testing: file exists (75776 bytes, CDFV2 Microsoft Excel format, owned by ga:ga). Copied from /workspace/data/sample_crf.xls by setup_task.sh Phase 4. Contains CRF named 'Vital Signs' version '1.0' with 7 items: systolic_bp (INT), diastolic_bp (INT), heart_rate (INT), temperature (REAL), resp_rate (INT), weight (REAL), height (REAL). All items are text input type in a single section 'Vital Signs Assessment'.

Verified from sample_crf.xls analysis: CRF sheet CRF_NAME='Vital Signs'. The export_result.sh checks for crf WHERE LOWER(TRIM(name))='vital signs'. Confirmed no Vital Signs CRF exists after setup cleanup (check06_crfs.txt is empty).
Stated in task description. The export_result.sh queries study WHERE parent_study_id=$STUDY_ID AND (unique_identifier='MRC-001' OR LOWER(name) LIKE '%memorial%').
Stated in task description as 'Protocol ID MRC-001'. Setup_task.sh Phase 2 cleans up any orphan MRC-001 site. Export checks unique_identifier='MRC-001'.
Stated in task description as 'Study Subject ID ONC-001'. The export_result.sh queries study_subject WHERE label='ONC-001'. Also referenced in the source document.
Stated in task description as 'Male'. OpenClinica stores gender as 'm' or 'f'. The export_result.sh captures sub.gender. Source document states '59-year-old male', consistent.

Stated in task description as 'Date of Birth: 1965-08-14'. Source document says '59-year-old male' at screening date 01-Mar-2025, which is consistent (1965-08-14 to 2025-03-01 = 59 years old). The export_result.sh captures sub.date_of_birth.

Stated in task description as '03/01/2025'. Source document says 'Date: 01-Mar-2025'. The export_result.sh checks study_event.date_start. All three representations are consistent (2025-03-01 = 03/01/2025 = 01-Mar-2025).
Verified via live testing: file exists (1103 bytes, owned by ga:ga, readable). Created by setup_task.sh Phase 5. Content verified (check02_data_files.txt) — contains all 7 vital signs values.

Verified from source document content (check02_data_files.txt): 'Systolic Blood Pressure:   142   mmHg'. The export_result.sh checks item_data WHERE value='142'. Clinically plausible for a 59-year-old male with controlled hypertension on lisinopril.

Verified from source document: 'Diastolic Blood Pressure:   88   mmHg'. The export_result.sh checks item_data WHERE value='88'. Note: 88 could also match heart_rate for some patients, but export checks each value independently.
Verified from source document: 'Heart Rate:                  76   bpm'. The export_result.sh checks item_data WHERE value='76'.
Verified from source document: 'Temperature:               37.1   C'. The export_result.sh checks item_data WHERE value='37.1'. Normal body temperature.

Verified from source document: 'Respiratory Rate:            18   breaths/min'. The export_result.sh does NOT currently check this value (no has_resp_rate field in export JSON). The CRF template item name is 'resp_rate'.
Verified from source document: 'Weight:                    81.4   kg'. The export_result.sh checks item_data WHERE value='81.4'.
Verified from source document: 'Height:                   175.0   cm'. The export_result.sh checks item_data WHERE value='175.0'. Note: agent must enter '175.0' not '175' for the value check to pass.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic end-to-end study activation task for OpenClinica 3.13 Community Edition. The agent must create a new study with Unique Protocol ID 'ONC-301', Brief Title 'Beacon NSCLC Phase III', PI 'Dr. James Morrison', Sponsor 'Beacon Oncology Inc.', Protocol Type 'Interventional', Expected Enrollment '120', Facility 'Memorial Research Center' in Boston, Massachusetts, United States. Three study events must be defined: 'Screening' (Scheduled, non-repeating), 'Baseline Assessment' (Scheduled, non-repeating), 'Cycle 1 Day 1' (Scheduled, non-repeating). The Vital Signs CRF template at /home/ga/vital_signs_crf.xls must be uploaded — it creates a CRF named 'Vital Signs' version '1.0' with 7 text-input items: systolic_bp, diastolic_bp, heart_rate, temperature, resp_rate, weight, height in a single section 'Vital Signs Assessment'. The CRF must be assigned as required to all three events. Site 'Memorial Research Center' with Protocol ID 'MRC-001' must be created under the study. Subject ONC-001 (Male, DOB 1965-08-14) must be enrolled at the site. A Screening visit must be scheduled for 2025-03-01. The source document at /home/ga/source_docs/ONC001_screening.txt contains the vital signs to enter: Systolic BP 142, Diastolic BP 88, Heart Rate 76, Temperature 37.1, Respiratory Rate 18, Weight 81.4, Height 175.0. The CRF must be marked complete after data entry. The database starts clean: no ONC-301 study, no Vital Signs CRF, no MRC-001 site exist. Four studies already exist (Default Study id=1, DM-TRIAL-2024 id=3, CV-REG-2023 id=4, AP-PILOT-2022 id=5). Login credentials: root / Admin123!. Important: the agent may encounter a password reset page after login — entering the old password and leaving new password blank will skip it.
